Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The event log has three cases with sequences of activities Lets extract the traces sequences of activities for each case Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The log shows a main sequence RA then VF then AE then possibly a loop of RMI and VF then AD then SC then AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Looking at the traces Case 2 RA VF AE AD SC AC no RMI VF loop Case 1 RA VF AE RMI VF AD SC AC one iteration RMI then VF Case 3 RA VF AE RMI RMI VF AD SC AC two RMIs then VF

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the pattern is after AE we can either go directly to AD or we can have one or more RMIs then a VF then proceed

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no trace with multiple iterations of the RMI and VF block So it is just an optional block not a loop with multiple iterations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The structure is after AE there is an XOR choice either go directly to AD or do a block of one or more RMIs then VF then AD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That block can be modeled as a sequence loop for RMIs then VF Since we do not have a one or more operator we can do RMI then tau loop with RMI to get one or more RMIs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This fits all traces Case 2 takes tau path so RA VF AE AD SC AC Case 1 takes RMI path with one RMI then VF then AD SC AC Case 3 takes RMI path with two RMIs then VF then AD SC AC

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no other concurrency The model should be simple and precise

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The tree is sequence RA sequence VF sequence AE sequence XOR tau sequence sequence RMI loop tau RMI VF sequence AD sequence SC AC

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This tree covers the log and generalizes to any number of RMIs It balances fitness precision and simplicity It does not allow random concurrency or multiple iterations of the whole RMI and VF block which are not in the log so it is precise
